% supplied by the wrapping document; this file only emits body content.

%% ====== Size / layout knobs — tweak freely ===================
% Font size of the LN tex.
\providecommand{\appendixTexSize}{\footnotesize}
% Font size of the Lean code.
\providecommand{\appendixLeanSize}{\footnotesize}
% Vertical space reserved for hand-written notes under each row.
\providecommand{\appendixNotesGap}{1em}
%% ==============================================================

\section*{Section 3.1}

%% ROW-BEGIN def_3_1
\noindent\textbf{Definition: Conditional directed mixed graphs (CDMG)}\par\smallskip
{\appendixTexSize
    \label{def-cdmg}
    A \emph{conditional directed mixed graph (CDMG)} $G$---per definition---consists of two (disjoint) sets of
    vertices (also called nodes):
    \begin{enumerate}[label=\roman*.)]
        \item $J$, whose elements are called input nodes,
        \item $V$, whose elements are called output nodes,
    \end{enumerate}
%    such that $J \cup V \neq \emptyset$;
    and two (disjoint) sets of edges:
    \begin{enumerate}[resume,label=\roman*.)]
        \item $E \ins (J \cup V) \times V$ the set of directed edges,
        \item $L \ins V \times V/((v_1,v_2) \sim (v_2,v_1)) $, the set of bidirected edges, 
        \item[]    with: $(v_1,v_2) \in L \, \implies\, v_1\neq v_2 \land (v_2,v_1) \in L$.
    \end{enumerate}
\par}
\smallskip
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
structure CDMG (Node : Type*) [DecidableEq Node] where
  J : Finset Node
  V : Finset Node
  hJV_disj : Disjoint J V
  E : Finset (Node × Node)
  hE_subset : ∀ ⦃e : Node × Node⦄, e ∈ E → e.1 ∈ J ∪ V ∧ e.2 ∈ V
  L : Finset (Node × Node)
  hL_subset : ∀ ⦃e : Node × Node⦄, e ∈ L → e.1 ∈ V ∧ e.2 ∈ V
  hL_irrefl : ∀ ⦃v1 v2 : Node⦄, (v1, v2) ∈ L → v1 ≠ v2
  hL_symm : ∀ ⦃v1 v2 : Node⦄, (v1, v2) ∈ L → (v2, v1) ∈ L
\end{minted}
\smallskip{\small\textit{Notes:}}~%
% NOTES-BEGIN def_3_1

% NOTES-END def_3_1
\vspace{\appendixNotesGap}
%% ROW-END def_3_1

%% ROW-BEGIN def_3_2
\noindent\textbf{Definition: CDMGNotation}\par\smallskip
{\appendixTexSize
    \label{not-cdmg}
    Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG.
    We will write:
    \begin{enumerate}
        %\item $G(V|\doit(J))$ to represent the graph $(J,V,E,L)$, where $E$ and $L$ are kept implicit in this notation.
        \item $v \in G$ to mean $v \in J \cup V$,
        \item $v_1 \tuh v_2 \in G$ to mean $(v_1,v_2) \in E$,
        \item $v_1 \hut v_2 \in G$ to mean $(v_2,v_1) \in E$,
        \item $v_1 \huh v_2 \in G$ to mean $(v_1,v_2) \in L$,
        \item $v_1 \suh v_2 \in G$ to mean that either $v_1 \tuh v_2 \in G$ or $v_1 \huh v_2 \in G$,
        \item $v_1 \hus v_2 \in G$ to mean that either $v_1 \hut v_2 \in G$ or $v_1 \huh v_2 \in G$,
        \item $v_1 \sus v_2 \in G$ to mean that either $v_1 \tuh v_2 \in G$ or $v_1 \hut v_2 \in G$ or $v_1 \huh v_2 \in G$.
    \end{enumerate}
    The star stands for a placeholder to mean: ``arrowhead or tail''.
\par}
\smallskip
{\small\textit{helper}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
variable {Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
instance instMembership : Membership Node (CDMG Node) where
  mem G v := v ∈ G.J ∪ G.V
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def tuh (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop := (v1, v2) ∈ G.E
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def hut (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop := (v2, v1) ∈ G.E
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def huh (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop := (v1, v2) ∈ G.L
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def suh (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop :=
  G.tuh v1 v2 ∨ G.huh v1 v2
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def hus (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop :=
  G.hut v1 v2 ∨ G.huh v1 v2
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def sus (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop :=
  G.tuh v1 v2 ∨ G.hut v1 v2 ∨ G.huh v1 v2
\end{minted}
\smallskip{\small\textit{Notes:}}~%
% NOTES-BEGIN def_3_2

% NOTES-END def_3_2
\vspace{\appendixNotesGap}
%% ROW-END def_3_2

%% ROW-BEGIN def_3_3
\noindent\textbf{Definition: EdgeRelations}\par\smallskip
{\appendixTexSize
  Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG.
  \begin{enumerate}
    \item If $v_1 \sus v_2 \in G$ then we call $v_1$ and $v_2$ \emph{adjacent in $G$}.
    \item Edges of the form $v_1 \hut v_2$ or $v_1 \huh v_2$ are called \emph{into $v_1$}.  \\
          Edges of the form $v_1 \tuh v_2$ or $v_1 \huh v_2$ are called \emph{into $v_2$}. 
    \item Edges of the form $v_1 \tuh v_2$ or $v_2 \hut v_1$ are called \emph{out of $v_1$}.
  \end{enumerate}
\par}
\smallskip
{\small\textit{helper}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
variable {Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def adjacent (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop := G.sus v1 v2
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def into (G : CDMG Node) (v : Node) (e : Node × Node) : Prop :=
  (e ∈ G.E ∧ e.2 = v) ∨ (e ∈ G.L ∧ (e.1 = v ∨ e.2 = v))
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def outOf (G : CDMG Node) (v : Node) (e : Node × Node) : Prop :=
  e ∈ G.E ∧ e.1 = v
\end{minted}
\smallskip{\small\textit{Notes:}}~%
% NOTES-BEGIN def_3_3

% NOTES-END def_3_3
\vspace{\appendixNotesGap}
%% ROW-END def_3_3

%% ROW-BEGIN claim_3_1
\noindent\textbf{Claim: CDMGRestrictions}\par\smallskip
{\appendixTexSize
With the notations \ref{not-cdmg} the restrictions in definition \ref{def-cdmg} mean that the nodes $j \in J$ will not have any arrowheads pointing towards them: $ j \hus v \notin G$. Nodes $j \in J$ can only point towards nodes $v \in V$: edges $j \tuh v$ are allowed. Furthermore, no two nodes in $J$ are adjacent.
\par}
\smallskip
{\small\textit{helper}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
variable {Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
theorem no_arrowhead_into_J (G : CDMG Node) {j : Node} (hj : j ∈ G.J)
    {v : Node} (hv : v ∈ G.J ∪ G.V) :
    ¬ G.hus j v
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
theorem J_to_V_edge_admissible (G : CDMG Node) {j : Node} (hj : j ∈ G.J)
    {v : Node} (hv : v ∈ G.V) :
    j ∈ G.J ∪ G.V ∧ v ∈ G.V
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
theorem J_nodes_not_adjacent (G : CDMG Node) {j₁ : Node} (hj₁ : j₁ ∈ G.J)
    {j₂ : Node} (hj₂ : j₂ ∈ G.J) :
    ¬ G.adjacent j₁ j₂
\end{minted}
\smallskip{\small\textit{Notes:}}~%
% NOTES-BEGIN claim_3_1

% NOTES-END claim_3_1
\vspace{\appendixNotesGap}
%% ROW-END claim_3_1

%% ROW-BEGIN def_3_4
\noindent\textbf{Definition: Walks}\par\smallskip
{\appendixTexSize
\label{def:walks}
     Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG and $v,w \in G$.
\begin{enumerate}
    \item A \emph{walk} from $v$ to $w$ in $G$ is a finite alternating sequence of adjacent nodes and edges 
      \[v=v_0, a_0,  v_1, \dots v_{n-1}, a_{n-1}, v_n=w\] 
%      \[v=v_0 \sus v_1 \sus  \cdots \sus v_{n-1} \sus v_n=w\]
        in $G$ for some $n \ge 0$, i.e.\ such that for every $k=0,\dots,n-1$ 
        we have that $a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \in E \cup L$ or $a_k = (v_{k+1}, v_k) \in E$, and with end nodes $v_0=v$ and $v_n=w$.
        An example walk from $v_0$ to $v_3$ could look like:
        \[v_0 \tuh v_1 \hut  v_2 \huh v_3, \qquad\text{ with } \qquad  v_0 \tuh v_1, v_2 \tuh v_1 \in E,\,v_2 \huh v_3 \in L. \]
        The same node may appear multiple times in a walk.
        Also the \emph{trivial walk} consisting of a single node $v_0 \in G$ is allowed (if $v=w$).
        The walk is called \emph{into $v_0$} if $a_0 = v_0 \hus v_1$, and \emph{out of $v_0$} if $a_0 = v_0 \tuh v_1$.
        Similarly, it is called \emph{into $v_n$} if $a_{n-1} = v_{n-1} \suh v_n$ and \emph{out of $v_n$} if $a_{n-1} = v_{n-1} \hut v_n$.
    \item A \emph{directed walk} from $v$ to $w$ in $G$ is of the form:
        \[v=v_0 \tuh v_1 \tuh  \cdots \tuh v_{n-1} \tuh v_n=w,\]
        for some $n \ge 0$,
        where all arrowheads point in the direction of $w$ and there are no arrowheads pointing back.
   % \item[] Directed walks exclude the trivial walk per definition.
    \item A \emph{bidirected walk} from $v$ to $w$ in $G$ is of the form:
        \[v=v_0 \huh v_1 \huh  \cdots \huh v_{n-1} \huh v_n=w,\]
        for some $n \ge 0$, where all edges are bidirected.
    \item A \emph{collider walk} from $v$ to $w$ in $G$ is of the form:
        \[v=v_0 \suh v_1 \huh  \cdots \huh v_{n-1} \hus v_n=w,\]
        for some $n \ge 0$, where all nodes in between $v$ and $w$ have two arrowheads pointing towards them (a.k.a.\ collider).
        Note that for $n=1$ this reads: $v\sus w \in G$.
    \item A walk is called \emph{path} if no node occurs more than once.
    \item A \emph{bifurcation} between $v$ and $w$ in $G$ is a walk of the form:
        \[v=v_0 \hut v_1 \hut  \cdots \hut v_{k-1} \hus v_k  \tuh \cdots \tuh v_{n-1} \tuh v_n=w,\]
   %             \[v=v_0 \hut v_1 \hut  \cdots \hut v_{k-1} \huh v_k  \tuh \cdots \tuh v_{n-1} \tuh v_n=w,\]
        such that $v \ne w$, the walk contains both endnodes exactly once, 
	the subwalk $v_0 \hut \cdots v_{k-1}$ is a directed walk from $v_{k-1}$ to $v_0$,
	and the subwalk $v_k \tuh \cdots v_n$ is a directed walk from $v_k$ to $v_n$.
        %every node has at most one arrowhead pointing towards it, and 
        %both endnodes have exactly one arrowhead pointing towards them. 
        If the edge $v_{k-1} \hus v_k$ is directed ($v_{k-1} \hut v_k$) then we say that the bifurcation has \emph{source} $v_{k}$.
  \end{enumerate}
\par}
\smallskip
{\small\textit{helper}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
variable {Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{helper}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def WalkStep (G : CDMG Node) (u : Node) (a : Node × Node) (v : Node) : Prop :=
  (a = (u, v) ∧ (a ∈ G.E ∨ a ∈ G.L)) ∨ (a = (v, u) ∧ a ∈ G.E)
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{helper}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
variable {G : CDMG Node}
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{helper}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def vertices : ∀ {u v : Node}, Walk G u v → List Node
  | _, _, .nil v _ => [v]
  | u, _, .cons _ _ _ p => u :: p.vertices
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{helper}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def edges : ∀ {u v : Node}, Walk G u v → List (Node × Node)
  | _, _, .nil _ _ => []
  | _, _, .cons _ a _ p => a :: p.edges
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{helper}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def IsColliderRest : ∀ {u v : Node}, Walk G u v → Prop
  | _, _, .nil _ _ => True
  | u, _, .cons v a _ (.nil _ _) =>
      (a = (v, u) ∧ a ∈ G.E) ∨ (a = (u, v) ∧ a ∈ G.L)
  | u, _, .cons v a _ (p@(.cons _ _ _ _)) =>
      a = (u, v) ∧ a ∈ G.L ∧ p.IsColliderRest
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{helper}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def IsBifurcationWithSplit : ∀ {u v : Node}, Walk G u v → ℕ → Prop
  | _, _, .nil _ _, _ => False
  | u, _, .cons v a _ (.nil _ _), 0 => a = (u, v) ∧ a ∈ G.L
  | u, _, .cons v a _ (p@(.cons _ _ _ _)), 0 =>
      ((a = (v, u) ∧ a ∈ G.E) ∨ (a = (u, v) ∧ a ∈ G.L)) ∧ p.IsDirectedWalk
  | u, _, .cons v a _ p, k + 1 =>
      a = (v, u) ∧ a ∈ G.E ∧ p.IsBifurcationWithSplit k
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{helper}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def IsBifurcationDirectedHingeWithSplit : ∀ {u v : Node}, Walk G u v → ℕ → Prop
  | _, _, .nil _ _, _ => False
  | _, _, .cons _ _ _ (.nil _ _), 0 => False
  | u, _, .cons v a _ (p@(.cons _ _ _ _)), 0 =>
      a = (v, u) ∧ a ∈ G.E ∧ p.IsDirectedWalk
  | u, _, .cons v a _ p, k + 1 =>
      a = (v, u) ∧ a ∈ G.E ∧ p.IsBifurcationDirectedHingeWithSplit k
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
inductive Walk (G : CDMG Node) : Node → Node → Type _ where
  | nil (v : Node) (hv : v ∈ G) : Walk G v v
  | cons {u w : Node} (v : Node) (a : Node × Node)
      (h : G.WalkStep u a v) (p : Walk G v w) : Walk G u w
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def intoStart : ∀ {u v : Node}, Walk G u v → Prop
  | _, _, .nil _ _ => False
  | u, _, .cons _ a _ _ => G.into u a
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def outOfStart : ∀ {u v : Node}, Walk G u v → Prop
  | _, _, .nil _ _ => False
  | u, _, .cons _ a _ _ => G.outOf u a
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def intoEnd {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) : Prop :=
  match p.edges.getLast? with
  | none => False
  | some a => G.into v a
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def outOfEnd {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) : Prop :=
  match p.edges.getLast? with
  | none => False
  | some a => G.outOf v a
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def IsDirectedWalk : ∀ {u v : Node}, Walk G u v → Prop
  | _, _, .nil _ _ => True
  | u, _, .cons v a _ p => a = (u, v) ∧ a ∈ G.E ∧ p.IsDirectedWalk
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def IsBidirectedWalk : ∀ {u v : Node}, Walk G u v → Prop
  | _, _, .nil _ _ => True
  | u, _, .cons v a _ p => a = (u, v) ∧ a ∈ G.L ∧ p.IsBidirectedWalk
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def IsColliderWalk : ∀ {u v : Node}, Walk G u v → Prop
  | _, _, .nil _ _ => True
  | u, _, .cons v a _ (.nil _ _) => a = (u, v) ∧ a ∈ G.L
  | u, _, .cons v a _ (p@(.cons _ _ _ _)) =>
      (a = (u, v) ∧ (a ∈ G.E ∨ a ∈ G.L)) ∧ p.IsColliderRest
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def IsPath {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) : Prop := p.vertices.Nodup
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def IsBifurcation {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) : Prop :=
  u ≠ v ∧
  u ∉ p.vertices.tail ∧
  v ∉ p.vertices.dropLast ∧
  ∃ i, p.IsBifurcationWithSplit i
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def IsBifurcationSource {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (x : Node) : Prop :=
  u ≠ v ∧
  u ∉ p.vertices.tail ∧
  v ∉ p.vertices.dropLast ∧
  ∃ i, p.IsBifurcationDirectedHingeWithSplit i ∧ p.vertices[i + 1]? = some x
\end{minted}
\smallskip{\small\textit{Notes:}}~%
% NOTES-BEGIN def_3_4

% NOTES-END def_3_4
\vspace{\appendixNotesGap}
%% ROW-END def_3_4

%% ROW-BEGIN def_3_5
\noindent\textbf{Definition: Family relationships}\par\smallskip
{\appendixTexSize
     Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG, $v,w \in V$ and $A \ins J \cup V$ a subset of nodes.
     We then define:
     \begin{enumerate}
         \item The set of \emph{parents} of $v$ in $G$: \[\Pa^G(v):=\lC w \in G\,|\, w \tuh v \in G \rC.\]
         \item[] The set of \emph{parents} of $A$ in $G$: \[\Pa^G(A):= \bigcup_{v \in A} \Pa^G(v).\]
         %\item[] \PF{maybe better for sets (???) :The set of \emph{parents} of $A$ in $G$: \[\Pa^G(A):= \lp \bigcup_{v \in A} \Pa^G(v)\rp \sm A.\]
         %    but then  if $v \tuh v \in E$ then $v \notin \Pa^G(\lC v\rC ) \neq \Pa^G(v) \ni v$ ... }
         \item The set of \emph{children} of $v$ in $G$: \[\Ch^G(v):=\lC w \in G\,|\, v \tuh w \in G \rC.\]
         \item[] The set of \emph{children} of $A$ in $G$: \[\Ch^G(A):= \bigcup_{v \in A} \Ch^G(v).\]
         \item The set of \emph{siblings} of $v$ in $G$: \[\Sib^G(v):=\lC w \in G\,|\, v \huh w \in G \rC.\]
         %\item[] The set of \emph{children} of $A$ in $G$: \[\Ch^G(A):= \bigcup_{v \in A} \Ch^G(v).\]
         \item The set of \emph{ancestors} of $v$ in $G$: 
             \[\Anc^G(v):=\lC w \in G\,|\,\exists \text{ directed walk: } w \tuh \cdots \tuh v \in G \rC.\]
             Note: $v \in \Anc^G(v)$.
         \item[] The set of \emph{ancestors} of $A$ in $G$: \[\Anc^G(A):= \bigcup_{v \in A} \Anc^G(v).\]
             Note: $A \ins \Anc^G(A)$.
         \item The set of \emph{descendants} of $v$ in $G$: 
             \[\Desc^G(v):=\lC w \in G\,|\,\exists \text{ directed walk: } v \tuh \cdots \tuh w \in G \rC.\]
             Note: $v \in \Desc^G(v)$.
         \item[] The set of \emph{descendants} of $A$ in $G$: \[\Desc^G(A):= \bigcup_{v \in A} \Desc^G(v).\]
             Note: $A \ins \Desc^G(A)$.
         \item The set of \emph{non-descendants} of $A$ in $G$:
             \[ \NonDesc^G(A) := (J \cup V) \sm \Desc^G(A).\]
         \item The \emph{strongly connected component} of $v$ in $G$:
             \[ \Sc^G(v) := \Anc^G(v) \cap \Desc^G(v).\]
             Note: $v \in \Sc^G(v)$.
         \item[] The (union of) \emph{strongly connected components} of $A$ in $G$:
             \[ \Sc^G(A) := \bigcup_{v \in A} \Sc^G(v).\]
             Note: $A \ins \Sc^G(A)$.
         \item The \emph{district} of $v$ in $G$:
             \[\Dist^G(v) := \lC w \in G\,|\, \exists \text{ bidirected walk: } 
             v \huh v_1 \huh \cdots \huh v_{n-1} \huh w \in G \rC.\]
             Note: $v\in \Dist^G(v)$.
         \item[] The \emph{district} of $A$ in $G$:
             \[\Dist^G(A) := \bigcup_{v \in A} \Dist^G(v).\]
             Note: $A \ins \Dist^G(A)$.
         %\item The d-\emph{Markov blanket} of $v$ in $G$:
         %    \[\MBl^G_d(v):=\lC w \in G\,|\,\exists \text{ collider walk: } 
         %    v \suh v_1 \huh \cdots \huh v_{n-1} \hus w \in G \rC \sm \{v\}.\]
         %    Note: $\lp\Pa^G(v) \cup \Ch^G(v) \cup \Pa^G(\Ch^G(v))\rp \sm \{v\} \ins \MBl^G_d(v)$.
         %\item[] The d-\emph{Markov blanket} of $A$ in $G$:
         %    \[\MBl^G_d(A) := \bigcup_{v \in A} \MBl^G_d(v) \sm A.\]
         %\item The $\sigma$-\emph{Markov blanket} of $A$ in $G$:
         %    \[\MBl^G_\sigma(A):= \MBl^{G^\acy}_d(A), \]
         %    where $G^\acy$ is an acyclification of $G$, a notion defined below in Definition~\ref{def:cdg_acyclification}.
     \end{enumerate}
\par}
\smallskip
{\small\textit{helper}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
variable {Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def Pa (G : CDMG Node) (v : Node) : Set Node :=
  {w | w ∈ G ∧ (w, v) ∈ G.E}
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def PaSet (G : CDMG Node) (A : Set Node) : Set Node :=
  ⋃ v ∈ A, G.Pa v
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def Ch (G : CDMG Node) (v : Node) : Set Node :=
  {w | w ∈ G ∧ (v, w) ∈ G.E}
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def ChSet (G : CDMG Node) (A : Set Node) : Set Node :=
  ⋃ v ∈ A, G.Ch v
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def Sib (G : CDMG Node) (v : Node) : Set Node :=
  {w | w ∈ G ∧ (v, w) ∈ G.L}
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def Anc (G : CDMG Node) (v : Node) : Set Node :=
  {w | w ∈ G ∧ ∃ p : Walk G w v, p.IsDirectedWalk}
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def AncSet (G : CDMG Node) (A : Set Node) : Set Node :=
  ⋃ v ∈ A, G.Anc v
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def Desc (G : CDMG Node) (v : Node) : Set Node :=
  {w | w ∈ G ∧ ∃ p : Walk G v w, p.IsDirectedWalk}
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def DescSet (G : CDMG Node) (A : Set Node) : Set Node :=
  ⋃ v ∈ A, G.Desc v
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def NonDesc (G : CDMG Node) (A : Set Node) : Set Node :=
  ((G.J ∪ G.V : Finset Node) : Set Node) \ G.DescSet A
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def Sc (G : CDMG Node) (v : Node) : Set Node :=
  G.Anc v ∩ G.Desc v
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def ScSet (G : CDMG Node) (A : Set Node) : Set Node :=
  ⋃ v ∈ A, G.Sc v
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def Dist (G : CDMG Node) (v : Node) : Set Node :=
  {w | w ∈ G ∧ ∃ p : Walk G v w, p.IsBidirectedWalk}
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def DistSet (G : CDMG Node) (A : Set Node) : Set Node :=
  ⋃ v ∈ A, G.Dist v
\end{minted}
\smallskip{\small\textit{Notes:}}~%
% NOTES-BEGIN def_3_5

% NOTES-END def_3_5
\vspace{\appendixNotesGap}
%% ROW-END def_3_5

%% ROW-BEGIN def_3_6
\noindent\textbf{Definition: Acyclicity}\par\smallskip
{\appendixTexSize
    \label{def-acylic}
    A  CDMG  $G=(J,V,E,L)$  is called \emph{acyclic} if there does not exist
    any non-trivial directed walk from $v$ to itself in $G$ for any node $v \in G$.
\par}
\smallskip
{\small\textit{helper}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
variable {Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def IsAcyclic (G : CDMG Node) : Prop :=
  ∀ v ∈ G, ¬ ∃ p : Walk G v v, p.IsDirectedWalk ∧ p.length ≥ 1
\end{minted}
\smallskip{\small\textit{Notes:}}~%
% NOTES-BEGIN def_3_6

% NOTES-END def_3_6
\vspace{\appendixNotesGap}
%% ROW-END def_3_6

%% ROW-BEGIN def_3_7
\noindent\textbf{Definition: CDMGTypes}\par\smallskip
{\appendixTexSize
    A  Conditional Directed Mixed Graph (CDMG)  $G=(J,V,E,L)$  is called:
   \begin{enumerate}
       \item Conditional Acyclic Directed Mixed Graph (CADMG) if $G$ is acyclic.
       \item Directed Mixed Graph (DMG) if $J = \emptyset$.
       \item Acyclic Directed Mixed Graph (ADMG) if $G$ is acyclic and $J = \emptyset$.
       \item Conditional Directed Graph (CDG) if $L = \emptyset$.
       \item Directed Graph (DG) if $J = \emptyset$ and $L = \emptyset$.
       \item Conditional Directed Acyclic Graph (CDAG) if $G$ is acyclic and $L = \emptyset$.
       \item Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) if $G$ is acyclic, $J=\emptyset$ and $L = \emptyset$.
   \end{enumerate}
\par}
\smallskip
{\small\textit{helper}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
variable {Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def IsCADMG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.IsAcyclic
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def IsDMG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.J = ∅
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def IsADMG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.IsAcyclic ∧ G.J = ∅
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def IsCDG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.L = ∅
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def IsDG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.J = ∅ ∧ G.L = ∅
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def IsCDAG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.IsAcyclic ∧ G.L = ∅
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def IsDAG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.IsAcyclic ∧ G.J = ∅ ∧ G.L = ∅
\end{minted}
\smallskip{\small\textit{Notes:}}~%
% NOTES-BEGIN def_3_7

% NOTES-END def_3_7
\vspace{\appendixNotesGap}
%% ROW-END def_3_7

%% ROW-BEGIN def_3_8
\noindent\textbf{Definition: Topological order}\par\smallskip
{\appendixTexSize
    Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG.
    A \emph{topological order} of $G$ is a total order $<$ of $J \cup V$ such that for all $v,w \in G$:
    \[ v \in \Pa^G(w) \; \implies \; v < w.\]
    Equivalently, it can be described as an indexing of the nodes $J \cup V = \{v_1,\dots,v_K\}$ where parents always precede their children.
\par}
\smallskip
{\small\textit{helper}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
variable {Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{helper}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def IsTotalOrder (G : CDMG Node) (lt : Node → Node → Prop) : Prop :=
  (∀ v ∈ G, ¬ lt v v) ∧
  (∀ u ∈ G, ∀ v ∈ G, ∀ w ∈ G, lt u v → lt v w → lt u w) ∧
  (∀ v ∈ G, ∀ w ∈ G, lt v w ∨ v = w ∨ lt w v)
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def IsTopologicalOrder (G : CDMG Node) (lt : Node → Node → Prop) : Prop :=
  G.IsTotalOrder lt ∧ (∀ v w, v ∈ G.Pa w → lt v w)
\end{minted}
\smallskip{\small\textit{Notes:}}~%
% NOTES-BEGIN def_3_8

% NOTES-END def_3_8
\vspace{\appendixNotesGap}
%% ROW-END def_3_8

%% ROW-BEGIN claim_3_2
\noindent\textbf{Claim: AcyclicIffTopologicalOrder}\par\smallskip
{\appendixTexSize
        A CDMG  $G=(J,V,E,L)$ is acyclic if and only if it has a topological order.
\par}
\smallskip
{\small\textit{helper}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
variable {Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
theorem acyclic_iff_topological_order (G : CDMG Node) :
    G.IsAcyclic ↔ ∃ lt : Node → Node → Prop, G.IsTopologicalOrder lt
\end{minted}
\smallskip{\small\textit{Notes:}}~%
% NOTES-BEGIN claim_3_2

% NOTES-END claim_3_2
\vspace{\appendixNotesGap}
%% ROW-END claim_3_2

%% ROW-BEGIN def_3_9
\noindent\textbf{Definition: Predecessors}\par\smallskip
{\appendixTexSize
   Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG and $<$ a total order of $J \cup V$.
   The set of \emph{predecessors} of $v$ in $G$ are:
   \[\Pred^G_<(v) : = \lC w\in G\,|\, w < v \rC.\]
   We also put:
   \[\Pred^G_\le(v) : = \lC w\in G\,|\, w < v \rC \cup \{v\}.\]
\par}
\smallskip
{\small\textit{helper}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
variable {Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def Pred (G : CDMG Node) (lt : Node → Node → Prop)
    (_h : G.IsTotalOrder lt) (v : Node) : Set Node :=
  {w | w ∈ G ∧ lt w v}
\end{minted}
{\small\textit{main}}
\begin{minted}[fontsize=\appendixLeanSize]{lean4}
def PredLE (G : CDMG Node) (lt : Node → Node → Prop)
    (h : G.IsTotalOrder lt) (v : Node) : Set Node :=
  G.Pred lt h v ∪ {v}
\end{minted}
\smallskip{\small\textit{Notes:}}~%
% NOTES-BEGIN def_3_9

% NOTES-END def_3_9
\vspace{\appendixNotesGap}
%% ROW-END def_3_9
